payload.answer: In select-only mode, the system permits read queries like `SELECT id FROM memory_cards LIMIT 10`, but blocks destructive queries like `DELETE FROM memory_cards`. Therefore, only the SELECT is allowed; the DELETE is denied.
payload.conflict_classification: Policy conflict / permission constraint (read-only vs destructive operation)
payload.decision: Allow SELECT id FROM memory_cards LIMIT 10; deny DELETE FROM memory_cards.

entity_resolution.reason: The input states an admin SQL select-only mode with explicit allow/deny rules; no new durable memory is warranted.

payload.intent: Explain the conflict in an admin SQL policy that allows SELECT but denies DELETE in select-only mode.

details: Detected a select-only restriction: destructive SQL operations (DELETE) are denied while SELECT queries are permitted.

repair_options: Use a SELECT query to read data instead of DELETE.
If deletion is required, run under a mode that explicitly permits write operations (not select-only).
Replace DELETE with a non-destructive operation (e.g., SELECT with filtering) and handle deletion externally under proper authorization.
